model: World Model v11.1 (per-horizon loss normalized, inducing-token diffusion)
key_question: Did per-horizon loss normalization fix horizon collapse and improve calibration?

metric_definitions.rho: Spearman rank correlation (actual vs predicted growth). Higher=better, 1.0=perfect
metric_definitions.MdAE: Median Absolute Error as pct of home value. Lower=better
metric_definitions.MAE: Mean Absolute Error as pct. Lower=better
metric_definitions.CRPS: Continuous Ranked Probability Score (proper scoring rule). Lower=better
metric_definitions.coverage_80: Fraction of actuals inside 80pct PI. Target=80pct
metric_definitions.interval_score: Width of PI + penalty for misses. Lower=better
metric_definitions.PIT_ks: KS test on PIT histogram. Lower=better (0=perfectly uniform)
metric_definitions.pct_neg_growth: Fraction of predictions with negative growth
metric_definitions.fan_width_avg: Average fan width (p95-p05) as fraction. Wider=more uncertain
